extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau where the value deviates strongly from the baseline (either clearly above or clearly below) covering roughly the middle half of the time axis.
R2: In addition to the long plateau, there is a single short plateau of opposite deviation (a local “bump” or “dip”) near one end of the series.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau (clearly above baseline) located near the center of the time axis, with relatively flat low segments before and after it.
R2: Apart from this central high plateau, there are no other large bumps or dips; the rest of the series stays close to one common low baseline.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (clearly below baseline) located near the center of the time axis, with relatively flat high segments before and after it.
R2: In addition to the long low plateau, there is a single short, sharp dip that briefly drops well below the surrounding values, located near one end of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau whose level is lower than the surrounding baseline (deep “valley” between two higher regions).
R2: There is exactly one short excursion (either a spike above or a dip below the baseline) on one side of that long plateau, but not on both sides.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau clearly higher than the surrounding baseline (a single wide “hill” between two lower regions).
R2: Apart from the start and end transitions into/out of this plateau, there are no additional strong spikes or dips elsewhere.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long plateau clearly lower than the surrounding baseline (a single wide “valley” between two higher regions).
R2: There is at least one additional short, sharp dip (narrow valley) outside that long plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau where values are clearly below the baseline (deep valley) roughly in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: In addition to the long valley, there is a single short, pronounced peak (values clearly above the baseline) that occurs either well before or well after the valley.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau of high values (clear step up above baseline) located in the central region of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this central high plateau, values stay close to a medium baseline level without any additional strong peaks or valleys.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau of low values (deep valley) in the central region of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this central valley, there is at least one additional short, sharp valley (brief drop much lower than the surrounding baseline) near one end of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau near the middle that is clearly **lower** than the surrounding baseline level.
R2: There is a **single, short spike** on one side of that low plateau (either before or after it) that rises **above** the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau near the middle that is clearly **higher** than the surrounding baseline level.
R2: Apart from that single high plateau, the signal stays close to a roughly constant baseline (no additional large spikes or dips).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau near the middle that is clearly **lower** than the surrounding baseline level.
R2: There is a **single, sharp dip** on one side of that low plateau (either before or after it) that falls **below** the baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau that differs strongly from the baseline, but this plateau is not centered: it starts or ends closer to one side (left or right) so that the “high” (or “low”) segment occupies a clearly off‑center portion of the time axis.
R2: In addition, there is a shorter, opposite‑direction excursion (a brief peak if the long segment is low, or a brief dip if the long segment is high) located near the *other* side of the series, separated in time from the long plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series shows a single long high plateau (values clearly above baseline) roughly in the middle of the time axis, with baseline‑level segments of similar height before and after it.
R2: Outside this central high plateau, there are no other pronounced peaks or dips: the beginning and ending segments stay near a common baseline without sharp excursions.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (values clearly below baseline) roughly in the middle of the time axis, with higher baseline segments of similar height before and after it.
R2: In addition to the central low plateau, there is at least one extra sharp, narrow dip (value dropping markedly below the neighboring baseline) occurring in either the first or the last part of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long, flat “low” plateau in the middle (roughly indices 40–100) clearly below the baseline level at the start and end.
R2: There is a single “high” peak or short high plateau on one side of that low middle part (either before it or after it), clearly above the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a long, flat “high” plateau in the middle (roughly indices 40–100) clearly above the baseline level at the start and end.
R2: Aside from this central high plateau and the roughly equal low baselines before and after it, there are no other large peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long, flat “low” plateau in the middle (roughly indices 40–100) clearly below the baseline level at the start and end.
R2: There is an additional short, deep dip on one side of that long low middle part (either before it or after it), clearly below that middle plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau near the middle that is clearly lower than its average level (a broad “valley” in the center).
R2: In addition to this central valley, there is a single sharp spike (short peak) located clearly away from the middle (toward either the beginning or the end).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series contains one long plateau near the middle that is clearly higher than its average level (a broad “hill” in the center).
R2: Outside this central hill, both the beginning and end of the series stay near the same lower baseline level without extra sharp spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long, deep plateau near the middle that is clearly lower than its average level (a broad, deep “valley” in the center).
R2: In addition to this central valley, there is exactly one extra sharp, brief valley (deep dip) located clearly away from the middle (toward either the beginning or the end).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains a long central plateau that is clearly lower than the surrounding baseline (a “valley”), with values around 0.1–0.2 while the baseline is around 0.45–0.5.  
R2: On exactly one side of this valley (either left or right, but not both) there is a pronounced spike above the baseline (up to about 0.8–0.85), while the other side stays close to the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single broad plateau clearly higher than the baseline (a “hill”), where the middle segment rises to about 0.8–0.9 while the baseline is around 0.45–0.5.  
R2: Both before and after this high plateau the signal quickly returns to and stays near the same baseline level, with no additional large spikes or valleys.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The dominant structure is a long central plateau clearly lower than the baseline (a “valley”), with values around 0.1–0.2 while the baseline is around 0.45–0.5.  
R2: In addition to this main valley, there is at least one extra deep, narrow dip below the baseline on one or both sides of the valley (a spike downward reaching roughly 0.15–0.2) while the rest of the side segments stay near the baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau: values stay clearly above the baseline (≈0.5) or clearly below it (≈0.2) for a central segment covering roughly one third to one half of the time axis.  
R2: In addition to the long plateau, there is a single short, opposite-direction excursion (short high spike when the long plateau is low, or short low dip when the long plateau is high) located near one end (start or end) of the series.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau: values rise sharply from the baseline (≈0.5) to a higher level (≈0.8–0.9) and remain high for a central segment covering roughly one third to one half of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this high plateau (both before it starts and after it ends), values remain close to a flat baseline around 0.45–0.5 without any additional large spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a single long low plateau: values drop sharply from the baseline (≈0.45–0.5) to a lower level (≈0.1–0.2) and remain low for a central segment covering roughly one third to one half of the time axis.  
R2: In addition to this long low plateau, there is one short, deep dip below the baseline on one side of the plateau (typically near the beginning or the end), creating an extra narrow valley distinct from the long central low segment.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a level clearly different from the baseline (higher or lower) that lasts roughly from the middle to near the end of the window (about indices 40–110).
R2: The series also contains one short plateau of opposite sign (if the long one is high, the short one is low; if the long one is low, the short one is high) located near one edge of the window (early or late), with the rest of the time near a constant baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau (values noticeably above baseline) located centrally in the window (roughly indices 40–110).
R2: Outside this high plateau, both the beginning and the end of the series stay close to the same mid-level baseline without any additional strong peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (values clearly below baseline) located centrally in the window (roughly indices 40–110).
R2: In addition to this long low plateau, there is at least one extra short, sharp dip to low values near one edge of the window, while the rest of the series (start and end) stays near a common higher baseline.
